subject: art
description: MYP Arts includes Visual Arts, Performing Arts (Music, Dance, Drama, Film). Four criteria: Knowing & understanding, Developing skills, Thinking creatively, Responding.

domains.name: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts

nodes.name: 素描与观察
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-3 will be able to employ a range of drawing techniques (e.g., contour, gesture, tonal rendering) to accurately represent form, space, and proportion.
Students in MYP years 4-5 will be able to select and refine drawing skills purposefully to develop a personal visual language and support artistic intention in their body of work.

nodes.name: 绘画媒材
curriculum_points: Students will be able to demonstrate technical competence in applying various painting media (e.g., watercolor, acrylic, gouache) to explore color theory, texture, and compositional effects.
Students will be able to make informed choices about painting materials and techniques to convey specific moods, concepts, or cultural contexts in their artwork.

nodes.name: 雕塑与立体
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 2-4 will be able to manipulate a variety of materials (e.g., clay, wire, found objects) using additive, subtractive, and construction techniques to create three-dimensional forms.
Students will be able to apply an understanding of spatial relationships, scale, and structural integrity to design and realize sculptural artworks.

nodes.name: 版画
curriculum_points: Students will be able to execute basic printmaking processes (e.g., relief, monotype) to produce multiple images, demonstrating an understanding of registration, inking, and editioning.
Students will be able to utilize the unique qualities of printmaking (e.g., repetition, variation) to explore pattern, sequence, or social commentary in their visual communication.

nodes.name: 数字媒体
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 3-5 will be able to use digital tools and software for creating, manipulating, and presenting still and moving images, demonstrating digital literacy.
Students will be able to critically evaluate the role of digital media in contemporary art practice and society, applying this understanding to their own creative projects.

nodes.name: 艺术史
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and analyze key artworks, movements, and cultural contexts from different historical periods and global traditions.
Students will be able to investigate the connections between art historical knowledge and their own artistic development, using research to inform and contextualize their work.

nodes.name: 识谱
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-3 will be able to interpret and apply standard musical notation (pitch, rhythm, dynamics, articulation) in performance and composition tasks.
Students in MYP years 4-5 will be able to fluently read and notate musical ideas using appropriate conventions, facilitating collaboration and analysis.

nodes.name: 乐器演奏
curriculum_points: Students will be able to develop technical proficiency on a chosen instrument or voice, demonstrating control over tone, intonation, and expressive techniques.
Students will be able to perform repertoire from diverse genres and cultures with stylistic awareness, both as a soloist and as part of an ensemble.

nodes.name: 创作与编曲
curriculum_points: Students will be able to create original musical pieces by structuring and developing musical ideas (melody, rhythm, harmony, texture) using appropriate tools and technology.
Students will be able to apply knowledge of musical elements and structures to compose for specific purposes, audiences, or contexts, and reflect on their creative process.

nodes.name: 音乐体裁
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and describe the distinguishing characteristics, cultural origins, and historical development of a range of musical genres.
Students will be able to compare and contrast musical genres, evaluating how social, historical, and technological factors influence musical style and practice.

nodes.name: 表演基础
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-2 will be able to apply fundamental acting techniques (e.g., vocal projection, physical characterization, focus) in prepared and improvised scenarios.
Students will be able to use voice, movement, and gesture intentionally to create believable characters and communicate clear intentions on stage.

nodes.name: 剧目排练
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 3-5 will be able to analyze a scripted scene to make directorial and performance choices regarding character motivation, relationship, and staging.
Students will be able to collaborate effectively within an ensemble to rehearse, refine, and perform scenes, demonstrating responsiveness and stagecraft.

nodes.name: 戏剧史
curriculum_points: Students will be able to trace the development of theatrical forms, conventions, and practitioners from classical traditions to contemporary practice.
Students will be able to investigate how theatre reflects and influences the society and culture in which it is created, and apply this understanding to their own dramatic work.

domains.name: 回应与反思 / Responding & Reflection / Responding

nodes.name: 艺术家研究
curriculum_points: Students will be able to conduct in-depth research into the life, influences, techniques, and body of work of a selected artist, musician, or theatre practitioner.
Students will be able to critically analyze the work of studied practitioners to evaluate their impact and relevance, and draw connections to their own artistic practice.

nodes.name: 艺术评论
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use appropriate arts terminology to describe, analyze, interpret, and evaluate artworks and performances (their own and others').
Students will be able to engage constructively in critique sessions, providing and applying feedback to refine artistic work in relation to stated intentions and criteria.

nodes.name: 过程日志
curriculum_points: Students will be able to document their artistic process, research, experimentation, and reflections consistently in a journal, demonstrating the development of ideas over time.
Students will use the process journal to plan, record, and evaluate their work against the MYP Arts criteria, showing evidence of critical thinking and artistic growth.
